# Abstract3D

> Local-first 3D generation for AbstractFramework, with a validated textured TripoSR-based `scene3d` plugin for AbstractCore, an experimental local Step1X geometry backend, an experimental license-gated Hunyuan3D shape backend (`abstract3d:hunyuan3d21-local`, serving `tencent/Hunyuan3D-2.1` and the multi-view `tencent/Hunyuan3D-2mv`; requires ABSTRACT3D_HUNYUAN_ACCEPT_LICENSE=1; the Tencent license excludes EU/UK/South Korea), and a shared projection texture bake (`abstract3d.texturing`; canonical orthographic or perspective projection with strict first-surface visibility, see ADR 0007).

## Document Index
- [README.md](README.md): overview, install, quick start, validated scope, and experimental Step1X status
- [docs/getting-started.md](docs/getting-started.md): setup and first successful runs
- [docs/architecture.md](docs/architecture.md): system shape and backend boundaries
- [docs/api.md](docs/api.md): Python and CLI reference
- [docs/methodology.md](docs/methodology.md): generation workflows, Apple-local operating profile, and evaluation criteria
- [docs/models.md](docs/models.md): validated backend, experimental Step1X path, blocked TRELLIS.2 path, and research catalog
- [docs/benchmarks.md](docs/benchmarks.md): checked proof assets, measured results, TripoSR texture proof, and TripoSR-vs-Step1X comparison
- [docs/integration-abstractcore.md](docs/integration-abstractcore.md): host integration
- [docs/faq.md](docs/faq.md): conceptual support answers
- [docs/troubleshooting.md](docs/troubleshooting.md): symptom-oriented fixes
- [docs/adr/README.md](docs/adr/README.md): durable engineering policy

---

## README.md

Abstract3D is a local-first 3D generation package for the AbstractFramework ecosystem.

It exposes a first-class `scene3d` capability for:

- `image_to_scene3d` (`i23d`)
- `text_to_scene3d` (`t23d`)

The validated default runtime path is:

- `stabilityai/TripoSR` for single-image 3D reconstruction
- lightweight `abstractvision` in the base install for provider-neutral composed `t23d`
- `glb` as the primary artifact contract, with `obj` and zipped bundles as secondary forms
- TripoSR defaults of `mc_resolution=256`, `cleanup=presentation`, baked `2048` base-color textures, and optional `mirror_symmetry` completion for single front-view symmetric subjects

The repository also ships an experimental Step1X geometry backend:

- backend id: `abstract3d:step1x-local`
- provider alias: `step1x`
- scope: official geometry checkpoint only
- `t23d`: composed `text -> image -> geometry`
- current status: works locally on Apple Silicon, but the checked proof cases were slower and lower-fidelity than TripoSR

Install profiles:

```bash
pip install abstract3d
pip install "abstract3d[triposr]"
pip install "abstract3d[step1x]"
pip install "abstract3d[hunyuan3d]"
pip install "abstract3d[apple]"
pip install "abstract3d[gpu]"
pip install "abstractcore[scene3d]"
```

`pip install abstract3d` includes the lightweight `abstractvision` package contract. Use
`abstract3d[apple]` or `abstract3d[gpu]` when the same environment should run local image
composition as well as local 3D backends.

Typical validated proof command:

```bash
python scripts/validate_local.py --backend triposr --device mps --mc-resolution 256
```

Typical validated image command:

```bash
abstract3d i23d ./object.png --output-dir ./out/object --device mps --format glb
```

That command now resolves to the cleaned textured TripoSR path by default. Use `--texture-mode vertex_color` for the lighter export, and `--texture-completion mirror_symmetry` when a single front-view symmetric subject needs better hidden-side texture.

The validated path is object-centric. It is not yet a general multi-object scene generator.

---

## docs/getting-started.md

Choose:

- `abstract3d[triposr]` for the validated `i23d` path
- `abstract3d[step1x]` for the experimental Step1X geometry path
- `abstract3d[apple]` for Apple-local `t23d` plus both local backend families
- `abstract3d[gpu]` for Linux/Windows GPU-local `t23d` plus both local backend families

Example commands:

- `abstract3d i23d ./object.png --output-dir ./out/object --device mps --format glb`
- `abstract3d i23d ./object.png --output-dir ./out/object-fast --device mps --format glb --texture-mode vertex_color`
- `abstract3d i23d ./object.png --output-dir ./out/object-step1x --backend step1x --model stepfun-ai/Step1X-3D --device mps --mc-resolution 128 --format glb`
- `abstract3d t23d "..." --output-dir ./out/teapot --device mps`
- checked Apple-local proof lane only: `abstract3d t23d "..." --output-dir ./out/teapot-proof --device mps --image-provider mlx-gen --image-model AbstractFramework/flux.2-klein-4b-8bit`

Measured local footprint from the checked Apple-local machine:

- TripoSR cache: `1.6G`
- pinned TripoSR source snapshot: `63M`
- Step1X geometry subset: `6.8G`
- pinned Step1X source snapshot: `147M`
- optional Apple-local proof image model cache (`AbstractFramework/flux.2-klein-4b-8bit` via `mlx-gen`): `8.0G`

Step1X uses `float32` on `mps`, and the texture stage is intentionally out of scope.

---

## docs/architecture.md

All supported paths enter through `Scene3DManager` or the AbstractCore `scene3d` facade, then route into the selected backend and the shared bundle writer.

Main backend roles:

- `TripoSRBackend`: validated default path, composed `t23d`, pinned TripoSR source snapshot, `glb`/`obj`/bundle export
- `Step1XGeometryBackend`: experimental geometry-only path, official Step1X geometry weights, composed `t23d`, Apple `mps` `float32`, local compatibility patch layer
- `Hunyuan3DShapeBackend`: experimental license-gated Hunyuan3D-2.1 shape path (official `tencent/Hunyuan3D-2.1` DiT + shape VAE, adaptive volume decoder, shared projection texture bake); requires `ABSTRACT3D_HUNYUAN_ACCEPT_LICENSE=1` because the Tencent license excludes the EU, UK, and South Korea
- `Trellis2LocalBackend`: experimental official-only path that remains outside the permissive validated default because of the required DINOv3 companion stack

Durable boundaries are governed by ADR 0001 through ADR 0005.

---

## docs/api.md

Public Python entry point: `Scene3DManager`.

Useful methods:

- `available_providers`
- `list_models`
- `list_operations`
- residency helpers
- `t23d`
- `i23d`
- `generate`
- `validate_suite`

Built-in backend ids:

- `abstract3d:triposr` / `triposr`
- `abstract3d:step1x-local` / `step1x`
- `abstract3d:hunyuan3d21-local` / `hunyuan3d21` / `hunyuan3d` / `hunyuan` (license-gated)
- `abstract3d:trellis2-local` / `trellis2`

The CLI provides:

- `catalog`
- `i23d`
- `t23d`
- `validate`

`--num-inference-steps` is exposed for Step1X generation and validation.

---

## docs/methodology.md

This page explains how the repository generates, measures, and reviews local 3D outputs.

Main workflows:

- validated `i23d` through TripoSR
- composed `t23d` through `abstractvision` image generation followed by the selected `i23d` backend
- experimental Step1X geometry-only `i23d`

Validated TripoSR texture policy:

- `mc_resolution=256`
- deterministic CPU cleanup before export
- baked base-color texture at `2048` by default
- `4096` only as an explicit hero-mode override

Apple-local operating profile:

- one heavy inference job at a time
- Step1X on `mps` `float32`
- conservative Step1X MPS cap
- Step1X runtime release before export and preview rendering
- CPU-side Step1X surface extraction and cleanup after denoising
- deterministic cleanup, vertex welding, and stable-pose export canonicalization

Primary review method:

- inspect `contact_sheet.png` first
- then inspect `metadata.json` for timings, mesh density, topology, RSS, and MPS allocation

Canonical public command patterns are documented for:

- `abstract3d i23d`
- `abstract3d t23d`
- `python scripts/validate_local.py`

---

## docs/models.md

Validated default backend:

- `stabilityai/TripoSR`
- tasks: `image_to_scene3d`, composed `text_to_scene3d`
- license: MIT
- Apple-local status: validated

Experimental Step1X backend:

- `stepfun-ai/Step1X-3D/Step1X-3D-Geometry-1300m`
- tasks: `image_to_scene3d`, composed `text_to_scene3d`
- license: Apache-2.0
- status: implemented and benchmarked on Apple Silicon
- boundary: geometry-only, official weights only, composed `t23d`, textured output out of scope

Experimental license-gated Hunyuan3D-2.1 backend:

- `tencent/Hunyuan3D-2.1/hunyuan3d-dit-v2-1`
- tasks: `image_to_scene3d`, composed `text_to_scene3d`
- license: tencent-hunyuan-community (territory-restricted: excludes the EU, UK, and South Korea)
- status: implemented and benchmarked on Apple Silicon; strongest geometry in the local catalog on the checked proof objects
- boundary: shape stage only, official weights only, explicit license acknowledgment required (`ABSTRACT3D_HUNYUAN_ACCEPT_LICENSE=1`), textures via the shared projection bake

Multi-view geometry through the same backend:

- `tencent/Hunyuan3D-2mv/hunyuan3d-dit-v2-mv` (`-fast` and `-turbo` subfolders accepted)
- select with `model="tencent/Hunyuan3D-2mv"`; same license gate as 2.1
- reference views whose angles snap to the trained front/left/back/right slots (25° tolerance) condition the shape; all references feed the texture bake
- metadata records `multiview_conditioning` and `geometry_views`

TRELLIS.2 remains implemented but not part of the permissive validated path because the official companion dependency stack still requires the gated non-permissive DINOv3 model.

---

## docs/benchmarks.md

Checked Apple-local benchmark profile:

- date: `2026-06-22`
- hardware: `Apple M5 Max`, `128 GB`
- Python: `3.12.13`
- device: `mps`
- checked Apple-local proof image provider: `mlx-gen`
- checked Apple-local proof image model: `AbstractFramework/flux.2-klein-4b-8bit`
- historical full-suite marching-cubes resolution: `128`
- current validated texture profile: `mc_resolution=256`, `cleanup=presentation`, `texture_mode=baked_basecolor`, `texture_resolution=2048`

Step1X-specific benchmark settings:

- backend id: `abstract3d:step1x-local`
- official model revision: `bf7084495b3a72222f36549b7942948aa4d9daa7`
- pinned source snapshot: `cb5ac944709c6c913109070c7b90c3447f57f3d4`
- Apple `mps` dtype: `float32`
- current published dynamic reference lane: `docs/assets/validation/local-step1x-dynamic/`

Aggregate comparison:

- TripoSR average total time: `6.3624 s`
- Step1X average total time: `101.8601 s`
- TripoSR average inference time: `0.4029 s`
- Step1X average inference time: `55.1116 s`
- TripoSR average MPS allocated: `1.5649 GiB`
- Step1X average MPS allocated: `0.0008 GiB`
- TripoSR average vertices/faces: `10,742` / `21,400`
- Step1X average vertices/faces: `57,146` / `104,932.5`

Interpretation:

- TripoSR remains the validated default because it was much faster on the checked Apple-local profile and produced more recognizable object shapes on the same cases.
- Step1X works locally, produces reproducible bundles, and integrates cleanly with AbstractCore, but it remains experimental.
- The focused rocket `i23d` comparison also favored TripoSR over the stable Apple-local Step1X label profile on the checked machine.
- A focused TripoSR texture proof now compares regular vertex-color output against baked `2048` textured output on two direct `i23d` objects and one `t23d`-derived object source.
- A focused TripoSR portrait proof also compares baseline baked texture, `mirror_symmetry`, and `mirror_symmetry` plus one auxiliary side reference for a single front-view portrait.

---

## docs/integration-abstractcore.md

Install `abstract3d` into the same environment as `abstractcore`.

Exposed backend ids:

- `abstract3d:triposr`
- `abstract3d:step1x-local`
- `abstract3d:trellis2-local`

Accepted provider aliases:

- `triposr`
- `step1x`
- `trellis2`

Example Step1X host call:

```python
mesh_step1x = llm.scene3d.i23d(
    "./object.png",
    provider="step1x",
    model="stepfun-ai/Step1X-3D",
    format="glb",
)
```

The current boundary is: TripoSR validated, Step1X geometry experimental, Hunyuan3D-2.1 license-gated and intentionally not registered with the AbstractCore plugin (direct `Scene3DManager`/CLI only), TRELLIS.2 implemented but outside the permissive validated path.

---

## docs/faq.md

Key answers:

- `t23d` is still composed, not native text-only 3D
- Step1X does not provide native text-to-3D in this repo
- Step1X textured output is not supported here
- TripoSR remains the validated default because the checked Apple-local proof favored it over Step1X
- neither the validated TripoSR path nor the experimental Step1X path requires CUDA in this repo
- the validated TripoSR path now emits baked textures by default, but atlas resolution cannot recover detail absent from the reconstructed TripoSR color field

---

## docs/troubleshooting.md

Main symptom-oriented fixes:

- install `abstract3d`, then configure an `abstractvision` provider, or use `abstract3d[apple]` / `abstract3d[gpu]` for local image composition if `t23d` fails
- compare against TripoSR if Step1X produces a dense blob or inflated mesh
- unset `ABSTRACT3D_STEP1X_SOURCE_DIR` and let the repo bootstrap the pinned official Step1X snapshot if source bootstrap is misconfigured
- review the Tencent license and set `ABSTRACT3D_HUNYUAN_ACCEPT_LICENSE=1` if the Hunyuan3D-2.1 backend refuses to run with a license acknowledgment error
- request official access or point `ABSTRACT3D_TRELLIS2_DINO_MODEL` at a valid local authorized snapshot if TRELLIS.2 fails on DINOv3
- retry on CPU, reduce `mc_resolution`, and keep one centered object if MPS behaves poorly
